commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9dd6623c7aaed736786369dbcfe35a6f3640562f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an optional `nonPersistableCommand` flag to the exec approval pending reply helper and helper-level tests for non-persistable versus `ask=always` Allow Always copy.

PR surface: Source +12, Tests +31. Total +43 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source with high confidence: current main collapses both `ask=always` and one-shot persistence to the same allowed-decision list, and the real callers forward only that list into the prompt helper. I did not run a live gateway or Telegram repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This PR needs contributor or maintainer review because it has a concrete runtime wiring defect and missing real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not queue automated repair markers while contributor proof is still required.

target.title: fix(exec-approval): distinguish non-persistable command from ask=always policy in approval prompt
target.body:
## Problem

When `allowAlwaysPersistence.kind === "one-shot"` (e.g. a command that uses shell redirection), `allow-always` is correctly excluded from the allowed decisions. However, the approval prompt showed the same message as when the exclusion is caused by an `ask=always` policy:

> "The effective approval policy requires approval every time, so Allow Always is unavailable."

This is misleading — the command can be approved, just not saved as a persistent pattern.

## Fix

Added an optional `nonPersistableCommand` flag to `ExecApprovalPendingReplyParams`. When the caller sets this to `true` (because the command isn't persistable), the prompt shows a distinct message:

> "Allow Always is unavailable because this command cannot be saved as a reusable pattern (it uses shell redirection or another non-persistable feature)."

The `ask=always` path keeps the original wording unchanged.

## Tests

Two new tests in `exec-approval-reply.test.ts`:
- `nonPersistableCommand: true` → non-persistable message, no policy message
- `ask: "always"` → policy message, no non-persistable message

```
Tests  34 passed (34)
```

Closes #97069

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
